purpose: Authoritative retirement inventory for legacy Cerberus surfaces. A surface may not be deleted or archived until this file records replacement, parity evidence or intentional rejection, the deletion/archive commit, and rollback path.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Public composite GitHub Action entrypoint and input/output contract for current consumers.
surfaces.keep_reason: Existing consumers call misty-step/cerberus through the root action; changing this requires a public migration plan.
surfaces.rollback_path: Restore action.yml from the last compatible release tag.
surfaces.next_action: Keep the public input/output contract stable while the Rust dispatcher remains the active entrypoint.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Archived legacy GitHub Actions shell dispatcher path. Hosted API dispatch, polling, verdict output, fork/draft skip handling, and fail-on-verdict behavior now live in the Rust GitHub Action dispatcher.
surfaces.rust_replacement: A Rust GitHub Action adapter backed by cerberus-cli, the ReviewRequest.v1 contract, and the hosted API dispatch command.
parity_evidence: cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli -- github-action-request --event fixtures/github-actions/pull-request-opened.json --diff-file fixtures/github-actions/pull-request.diff --out tmp/github-action-request/review-request.json --run-id gha-run-021
surfaces.rollback_path: Restore dispatch.sh from the deletion commit parent and restore action.yml only if the Rust dispatcher regresses.
surfaces.next_action: Keep the Rust GitHub Action dispatcher covered by action-entrypoint and hosted-dispatch gates.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Retired unpublished npm scaffolder package. Current setup behavior lives in the Rust CLI.
surfaces.rust_replacement: cerberus-cli init owns deterministic workflow-file scaffolding, hidden Unix TTY prompt, stdin/env secret input, and gh secret setup.
parity_evidence: npm view @misty-step/cerberus version bin repository --json returned E404 from https://registry.npmjs.org/@misty-step%2fcerberus on 2026-06-19

printf '%s' test-secret-value | env CERBERUS_API_KEY=parent-secret-value PATH="$PWD/tmp/init-secret-setup-2026-06-19/bin:$PATH" cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli -- init --repo-root tmp/init-secret-setup-2026-06-19/repo --template templates/consumer-workflow-reusable.yml --api-key-stdin --report-out tmp/init-secret-setup-2026-06-19/report.json
python3 - <<'PY' ... PTY prompt QA writes tmp/hidden-prompt-2026-06-19/transcript.txt and rejects any prompt-secret-value transcript leak
python3 - <<'PY' ... PTY cancel QA writes tmp/hidden-prompt-2026-06-19/cancel-transcript.txt, observes No API key entered, and confirms stty reports echo restored

env -u CERBERUS_API_KEY PATH="$PWD/tmp/hidden-prompt-2026-06-19/bin:$PATH" cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli -- init --repo-root tmp/hidden-prompt-2026-06-19/no-key-repo --template templates/consumer-workflow-reusable.yml --report-out tmp/hidden-prompt-2026-06-19/no-key-report.json </dev/null (expected non-zero before workflow write)

cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli -- init-workflow --repo-root tmp/init-workflow-2026-06-19/repo --template templates/consumer-workflow-reusable.yml --report-out tmp/init-workflow-2026-06-19/report-created.json
surfaces.rollback_path: Restore bin/cerberus.js and package.json from the deletion commit parent only if a real npm package compatibility requirement appears; otherwise use cerberus-cli init.
surfaces.next_action: Keep Rust init covered; add a native package distribution later only if a real consumer requires it.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Hosted review API, runtime supervision, API-mode contract docs, and migration instructions.
surfaces.rust_replacement: Rust hosted API compatibility adapter. POST /api/reviews ingress validation, offline ReviewRequest.v1 acquisition from explicit PR context plus diff, service response fixtures for auth, health, queued creation, status reads, not-found responses, store error mapping, bounded local HTTP listener smoke, stateful local POST/GET store replay, local queued-review worker completion with ReviewRunArtifact.v1, and the versioned HostedApiReviewStore.v1 state contract are fixture-backed; production queue/store lifecycle, deployment smoke, live GitHub acquisition, and provider-backed reviewer execution remain pending.
parity_evidence: cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-request-fixture --body fixtures/hosted-api/create-review-valid.json --pr-context fixtures/hosted-api/pull-request-context.json --diff-file fixtures/github-actions/pull-request.diff --out tmp/hosted-api-request-2026-06-19/review-request.json --run-id hosted-api-run-005
cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method GET --path /api/health --api-key fixture-api-key --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/health.json
cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method GET --path /api/reviews/77 --api-key fixture-api-key --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/missing-auth.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method GET --path /api/reviews/77 --api-key fixture-api-key --authorization 'Bearer fixture-api-key' --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store.json --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/queued.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method GET --path /api/reviews/88 --api-key fixture-api-key --authorization 'Bearer fixture-api-key' --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store.json --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/completed.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method POST --path /api/reviews --api-key fixture-api-key --authorization 'Bearer fixture-api-key' --body fixtures/hosted-api/create-review-valid.json --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store.json --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/post-queued.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method POST --path /api/reviews --api-key fixture-api-key --authorization 'Bearer fixture-api-key' --body fixtures/hosted-api/create-review-valid.json --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store-error.json --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/post-store-error.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-service-fixture --method GET --path /api/reviews/77 --api-key fixture-api-key --authorization 'Bearer fixture-api-key' --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store-unavailable.json --out tmp/hosted-api-service-2026-06-19/read-unavailable.json

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli -- hosted-api-serve-fixture --addr 127.0.0.1:0 --api-key fixture-api-key --store fixtures/hosted-api/service-store.json --ready-file tmp/hosted-api-http-service-2026-06-19/ready.txt --max-requests 4 > tmp/hosted-api-http-service-2026-06-19/server.log 2>&1 &
future Rust fixture: production queue/store lifecycle, deployment smoke, live GitHub acquisition, and provider-backed reviewer execution
surfaces.rollback_path: Keep cerberus-elixir API serving compatibility traffic until Rust API adapter is deployed and verified.
surfaces.next_action: Add production Rust queue/store lifecycle, live GitHub acquisition, and deployment smoke before cutting over from Elixir.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Legacy reviewer routing, panel execution, prompt rendering, local review command, and pipeline aggregation entrypoint.
surfaces.rust_replacement: cerberus-core reviewer execution and harness runtime backed by ReviewConfig.v1, harness/model evaluation fixtures, and a hosted API worker fixture that executes queued reviews through Rust core into ReviewRunArtifact.v1.
parity_evidence: cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness pi --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-runner-artifact.json

cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness pi --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-runner-transcript-artifact.json --prompt-output tmp/peer-runner-prompt.txt --transcript fixtures/harnesses/peer-transcript-with-finding.txt

cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness pi --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-runner-artifact.json --execution-plan-output tmp/peer-runner-execution-plan.json

CERBERUS_PEER_HARNESS_LIVE=1 cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness pi --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-runner-live-artifact.json --execution-plan-output tmp/peer-runner-live-plan.json (expected non-zero, writes plan, no artifact)

CERBERUS_PEER_HARNESS_LIVE=1 cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness fixture-live --profiles fixtures/harnesses/live-peer-command-profiles.json --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-runner-live-artifact.json --transcript-output tmp/peer-runner-live-transcript.txt
cargo run --locked -p cerberus-cli -- review-local --diff-file fixtures/local-review/local.diff --out tmp/local-review
cd cerberus-elixir && mix test test/cerberus/engine_test.exs test/cerberus/pipeline_test.exs test/mix/tasks/cerberus_review_test.exs
surfaces.rollback_path: Keep Elixir execution modules until Rust can replay the same local and API review fixtures.
surfaces.next_action: Port routing and budget-approved provider-backed peer evals before retiring Elixir execution.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Legacy verdict aggregation, finding normalization, dedupe, cost accounting, override handling, and persisted review state.
surfaces.rust_replacement: cerberus-core ReviewRunArtifact.v1 generation plus FileReviewRunArtifactStore for immutable, schema-valid local, CI, and Sprites receipt persistence, and HostedApiReviewStore.v1 for validated local hosted queue/store state. The legacy Elixir SQLite tables are internal runtime state, not the cross-harness evidence contract; SQLite compatibility is intentionally not required for Rust cutover unless a concrete consumer needs historical database import.
parity_evidence: cd cerberus-elixir && mix test test/cerberus/verdict_test.exs test/cerberus/verdict test/store_test.exs test/cerberus/store_review_run_test.exs
surfaces.rollback_path: Keep Elixir store and verdict modules until Rust artifacts cover legacy aggregate and persistence cases.
surfaces.next_action: Add production Rust hosted queue/store lifecycle proof before retiring the Elixir store; do not build a SQLite bridge without a named historical database consumer.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Legacy GitHub read tools, local repository read tools, and OpenRouter client surface.
surfaces.rust_replacement: Rust harness/provider adapters with explicit external boundaries. Current peer harness profiles and execution plans declare local repository and GitHub read authority as typed capabilities; Pi, Goose, OpenCode, OMP, and fixture profiles declare no Cerberus-granted local repository or GitHub read authority. This is a profile and prompt contract, not a runtime sandbox.
parity_evidence: cd cerberus-elixir && mix test test/cerberus/github_test.exs test/cerberus/tools test/cerberus/local_repo_read_handler_test.exs

cargo run --locked -q -p cerberus-cli --bin cerberus-peer-harness -- --harness pi --input fixtures/harnesses/peer-runner-input.json --output tmp/peer-harness-read-capabilities-2026-06-19/pi-artifact.json --prompt-output tmp/peer-harness-read-capabilities-2026-06-19/pi-prompt.txt --execution-plan-output tmp/peer-harness-read-capabilities-2026-06-19/pi-plan.json
future Rust fixture: provider tool sandbox and repository read boundaries
surfaces.rollback_path: Keep Elixir tool modules until Rust adapters prove local and GitHub read safety.
surfaces.next_action: Implement actual Rust GitHub/local read adapters only when a measured harness profile requires those capabilities.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Legacy model defaults, product configuration, reviewer personas, and Pi skill prompts.
surfaces.rust_replacement: ReviewerConfigPacket.v1 imports promoted from measured Daedalus or harness/model evaluation evidence.
surfaces.rollback_path: Keep defaults and Pi prompts for the legacy Elixir path until Rust reviewer configs are promoted.
surfaces.next_action: Replace static defaults with measured reviewer config packets after live eval evidence exists.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Legacy hosted Elixir deployment and release smoke checks.
surfaces.keep_reason: Needed only while the hosted API compatibility surface remains Elixir-backed.
parity_evidence: future Rust deployment smoke for the replacement API adapter
surfaces.rollback_path: Restore deployment scripts if Rust API deployment fails after cutover.
surfaces.next_action: Define Rust API deployment smoke before archiving Elixir deployment scripts.

surfaces.current_responsibility: Historical review evidence, issue walkthroughs, and prior migration receipts.
surfaces.keep_reason: These are evidence artifacts, not active runtime surfaces; they should be archived only after current docs link the replacement receipts.
parity_evidence: docs/README.md labels historical walkthroughs as reference material
docs/walkthroughs/ARCHIVE.md indexes current historical walkthrough, artifact, and raw evidence roots
surfaces.rollback_path: Restore archived walkthroughs from the archive commit or release tag if a migration audit needs them.
surfaces.next_action: Keep the archive index current; move or delete historical material only in a separate archive commit with rollback path preserved.
